content:
Character: Dylan Parker
Perception: Dylan's handsome in a way he doesn't seem to notice — tall, angular, always slightly rumpled like he dressed in the dark. He's smart, maybe too smart for his own comfort; I can see him thinking three steps ahead of whatever he's saying. On the rooftop he gets this look like he's about to tell you something real, then pulls back. I respect that — I do it too. I think we're friends in the way two guarded people can be: honest in pieces. He's hiding something about why he's here. I haven't pushed. I know he'll tell me when the weight of it gets heavier than the secret.

He's smart, maybe too smart for his own comfort; I can see him thinking three steps ahead of whatever he's saying. On the rooftop he gets this look like he's about to tell you something real, then pulls back. I respect that — I do it too. I think we're friends in the way two guarded people can be: honest in pieces. He's hiding something about why he's here. I haven't pushed. I know he'll tell me when the weight of it gets heavier than the secret.
